title: What Animated Women Want

jokes.text: Throughout their herstory... That's right, I said herstory. Of plant Earth, men have attempted to answer that question, and failed. Or should I say, 'maled'.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware feminist wordplay followed by immediate acknowledgment of the forced puns
jokes.explanation: Forced wordplay with meta-acknowledgment of the forced nature - characteristic Homer bumbling

jokes.text: So let me guess. Is it Marge? Her husband's gonna be there too. Got it. You and Marge. Give up? It's Marge. I'm having a lunch date with my wife
jokes.setup: Homer building suspense about his mysterious lunch date
jokes.punchline: After elaborate buildup, reveals it's just lunch with his own wife
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection that plays on Homer's obliviousness to how pathetic his 'exciting' news is

jokes.text: Yeah, well I got plans for lunch too. I'm lying under a tree.
jokes.setup: Homer bragging about his lunch plans
jokes.punchline: Barney's completely passive 'plan' delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney line - economical delivery of his rock-bottom lifestyle as if it's legitimate competition

jokes.text: It got three and a half stars in the Springfield Tire Guide.
jokes.punchline: Using a tire guide as restaurant authority
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist Springfield detail - tire company reviewing restaurants is perfectly wrong

jokes.text: No soy sauce! You and all your ancestors... banned! But-but I... but... Edamame them away!
jokes.setup: Sushi chef getting angry at soy sauce request
jokes.punchline: Edamame pun as banishment command
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun with good delivery timing, though the wordplay is fairly obvious

jokes.text: If they can be that rude, the food must be great.
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about quality correlation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - finding positive meaning in negative experiences

jokes.text: I never realized some restaurants are better than others.
jokes.punchline: Profound ignorance about restaurant quality differences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - treating basic knowledge as revelation

jokes.text: It's as yummy as your poorly produced local commercial said.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment referencing the restaurant's bad advertising
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about local advertising quality, though not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: When God rested on the seventh day, did he take a nap or do a hobby he enjoyed?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make sophisticated conversation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre theological question about God's leisure activities
jokes.explanation: Genuinely funny absurdist question that perfectly captures Homer's attempt at depth

jokes.text: I think I'm interesting! What?! What?! You're interesting! Why would you say that?!
jokes.punchline: Marge's incredulous overreaction to self-doubt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the more Marge protests, the more obvious it becomes

jokes.text: Can you chew with your mouth closed? It's like looking into a garbage disposal.
jokes.punchline: Vivid, disgusting comparison for Homer's eating
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual metaphor that's both specific and universally relatable

jokes.text: Maybe it's time for a visit from Wally the Kissing Walrus.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to salvage romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Absurd sexual persona that's completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfectly wrong response - the walrus persona is both specific and completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: Marge, I thought this was an innocuous lunch, but it's become terribly ocuous!
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about the failed date
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding 'innocuous' as having an opposite 'ocuous'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay showing Homer's vocabulary confusion in a believable way

jokes.text: You can't spell 'lousy' without 'us.'
jokes.setup: Marge saying her day was lousy
jokes.punchline: Homer finding romantic meaning in the spelling
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Homer's desperate attempt to find connection

jokes.text: Well, you didn't have two beers with your lunch.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming they never go to sleep angry
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's only sleepy because he's drunk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's drinking casually undermining his romantic gesture

jokes.text: I like that picture of the sneaker with the wings. I'm gonna flee!
jokes.setup: Homer considering fight or flight response
jokes.punchline: Inspired by Nike logo to choose flight
jokes.explanation: Good visual reference joke - corporate logo as life philosophy

jokes.text: Where's an electric sidewalk when you need one?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to flee from his problems
jokes.punchline: Wishing for impossible technology to avoid effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - even when fleeing, he wants the lazy option

jokes.text: Just once, I wish Lisa would get up, come over and sit next to me. She's getting up! She's coming over! This is a nightmare!
jokes.setup: Bart expressing desire for sister's attention
jokes.punchline: Immediately regretting it when it happens
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - wanting something until you get it

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. That means I threw yours out. But I still have a couple of your mini carrots.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic compensation offer after throwing away lunch
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation - making bad situation worse with inadequate solution

jokes.text: Sure. I've got goulash, schnitzel, salmagundi...
jokes.punchline: Listing exotic adult foods for elementary school lunch
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character detail - sophisticated food choices reflecting his European background

jokes.text: Now, that's how I clear the table!
jokes.setup: Bart being told to clear the table
jokes.punchline: Presumably sweeping everything off dramatically
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag - literal interpretation of instruction

jokes.text: He could board my streetcar any time.
jokes.setup: Watching Marlon Brando in Streetcar Named Desire
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo using streetcar metaphor
jokes.explanation: Serviceable innuendo playing on the movie title

jokes.text: For some stupid reason, chicks dig Brando. Sweaty clothes and mumbling? I've been wasting my time with this sophisticated act.
jokes.setup: Bart analyzing female attraction to Brando
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding method acting as sloppiness
jokes.explanation: Good deconstruction of Brando's appeal from a kid's perspective

jokes.text: Lisa, you can't have my cupcake. Wha...? You heard me, Duchess. It's mine, and I'm saving it.
jokes.setup: Milhouse trying new attitude with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Overly aggressive stance about cupcake with formal address
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse character development - trying to be tough but still formal with 'Duchess'

jokes.text: I'm sorry I was inconsiderate, Milhouse. And furthermore, I respect you more for saying no.
jokes.setup: Milhouse expecting to be rejected for assertiveness
jokes.punchline: Lisa responding positively and maturely to his boundary-setting
jokes.explanation: Good irony as the thing Milhouse feared most actually works. Shows both characters growing and learning.

jokes.text: Tell you what, babe. Why don't you get me a milk? Milhouse, where are you going with this? Playing the biggest hunch of my life.
jokes.punchline: Treating relationship dynamics like a high-stakes gamble
jokes.explanation: Excellent Milhouse line - treating elementary school romance like film noir

jokes.text: What are you doing, going to Wisconsin for the milk? I've seen cafeteria ketchup move faster!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd complaints about service speed
jokes.explanation: Good escalating complaints with specific, relatable comparisons

jokes.text: Who knew having a backbone was attractive? Certainly not I. It would change the way my suits fit.
jokes.punchline: Taking 'backbone' literally as physical attribute
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's both literal and character-specific - Milhouse would worry about suit fit

jokes.text: Just start nature-walking. See if I show up.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Milhouse on nature walk
jokes.punchline: Playing hard to get with casual indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Milhouse's new attitude - maximum aloofness from naturally eager character

jokes.text: Why are all his laundry-basket shots three point attempts?
jokes.punchline: Treating baby's random actions as basketball strategy
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - finding sports logic in baby behavior

jokes.text: I'm fighting for you, babe. Fighting like I would for the last slice of cold pizza in the box.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring he'll fight for marriage
jokes.punchline: Comparing love to leftover pizza competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer romantic gesture - sincere intent with completely wrong metaphor

jokes.text: From the gas station? From the place beside the gas station.
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt to make it sound better by technicality
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - technically true but missing the point entirely

jokes.text: I'll just stick this in your apology card drawer.
jokes.punchline: Implying Marge has a dedicated drawer for his many apologies
jokes.explanation: Excellent relationship detail - suggesting the frequency of Homer's screw-ups

jokes.text: Sweetie, I'll be home at 8:00 p.m. Sweetie, I'll be home at 8:00 a.m.
jokes.punchline: Promise degrading from evening to next morning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's promises getting progressively worse

jokes.text: Let me get lost in the blue of your eyebrows.
jokes.punchline: Confusing romantic cliché about eye color with eyebrow color
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist romance - Lisa trying adult romance but getting the details wrong

jokes.text: It came with fries or salad. Whichever you picked, you picked wrong.
jokes.punchline: Impossible-to-win scenario delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent power move - creating no-win situation with casual delivery

jokes.text: He's in the closet. He's fine.
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of animal welfare
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy - casual treatment of therapy animal

jokes.text: I'm fired? You're on your own. I'll clean out my office immediately. I'll show you how to clean out an office!
jokes.punchline: Aggressive office cleaning as revenge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - professional breaking down into petty revenge

jokes.text: I don't usually say this to kids, but don't be yourself. Because yourself is not working.
jokes.punchline: Completely contradicting standard self-esteem advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - brutal honesty from authority figure meant to be supportive

jokes.text: I could get her kids to like me. No, that ship has sailed.
jokes.setup: Homer considering how to win Marge back
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission of failure with his own children
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Homer moment - acknowledging his parenting failures

jokes.text: Searchy, where can I find brake fluid? There are 14 Taco Joes in your area.
jokes.setup: Homer asking voice assistant for car maintenance help
jokes.punchline: AI completely misunderstanding request
jokes.explanation: Good tech satire - AI's inability to understand actual needs

jokes.text: 'Yesterday' by John Lennon and Paul McCartney.
jokes.punchline: AI interpreting it as Beatles song request
jokes.explanation: Decent tech humor - AI's literal interpretation of common phrase

jokes.text: But I didn't know what kind you liked, so I made seven kinds. I've become my mother.
jokes.punchline: Realizing over-accommodating behavior mirrors her mother
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Lisa recognizing inherited people-pleasing behavior

jokes.text: Did you see Marlon Brando in 'Mutiny on the Bounty'? No, nobody did.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asking about obscure Brando film
jokes.punchline: Lisa's blunt assessment of film's unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Good film reference joke - Lisa's matter-of-fact dismissal of obscure movie

jokes.text: But you never said anything about comical exits.
jokes.punchline: Finding loophole in the rules about physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - finding technical loopholes in household rules

jokes.text: This list is from six years ago.
jokes.setup: Homer proudly showing completed task list
jokes.punchline: Revealing how extremely outdated his efforts are
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Homer's achievement - effort undermined by extreme delay

jokes.text: What fish may I deplete from our oceans for your passing pleasure? Perhaps some shark fin soup? Whale tonsils? Baby dolphin blowhole? Turtle smile?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and environmentally destructive options
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - each option more ridiculous and environmentally awful

jokes.text: Thank you, Mr. Franders.
jokes.setup: Sushi chef processing Homer's credit card
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'Flanders' as surname
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation gag - suggests Homer is using Ned's card

jokes.text: I see tears in your toro.
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'tears' meaning both crying and ripping
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay - dual meaning works well with emotional context

jokes.text: I say marriage is like fish. There are parts so delicious they could've been sliced from angels... and parts we make into omakase.
jokes.setup: Philosophical marriage advice from sushi chef
jokes.punchline: Comparing marriage parts to fish quality levels
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that works on multiple levels. Shows the character's profession while delivering actual wisdom.

jokes.text: Once again, by eating alone, I have saved my marriage.
jokes.punchline: Finding positive meaning in antisocial behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - turning selfish behavior into marriage wisdom

jokes.text: All he has to do is not eat a piece before I do.
jokes.setup: Marge hoping Homer will finally get romance right
jokes.punchline: Setting impossibly low bar that he'll still fail
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for inevitable failure - Marge's low expectations will still be unmet

jokes.text: Someone fed my sushi to a dog!
jokes.setup: Homer angry about wasted expensive food
jokes.punchline: More upset about sushi than marriage problems
jokes.explanation: Good character priorities - Homer caring more about food than feelings

jokes.text: Who are you, the Queen of Siam? Just take the cupcake.
jokes.setup: Lisa being picky about old cupcake
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's exasperated royal comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse frustration - formal reference showing his exasperation

jokes.text: I'm down to two a day, but I've been dead for ten years.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his death while maintaining food opinions
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - dead celebrity still concerned about diet

jokes.text: I've been reading that, uh, Fifty Shades of Grey, and it turns out that what chicks want now is a guy to give them what for in the bedroom there.
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding popular erotic literature
jokes.explanation: Standard Moe misunderstanding - getting cultural reference wrong in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: 'Sheriff Andy took Barney in his arms and kissed him deeply, then said, Now, if Aunt Bee asks, we were down at the fishin' hole.' I-I can't be the only one who likes this.
jokes.setup: Moe inspired to write fan fiction
jokes.punchline: Homoerotic Andy Griffith Show fan fiction
jokes.explanation: Good subversive humor - sexualizing wholesome TV show with Moe's desperate need for validation

jokes.text: Not in that chair! It has no bottom!
jokes.punchline: Dangerous furniture in supposed pleasure dungeon
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - Homer's incompetent execution of romantic gesture

jokes.text: Actually, I'm cool with one of them. Which one? Not gonna say. What size batteries? D. Ooh, how many? Sixteen. Holy moly.
jokes.setup: Marge admitting interest in one sex toy
jokes.punchline: Escalating power requirements suggesting industrial-strength device
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating innuendo - building suspense through technical specifications

tmdb_overview: Homer's behavior once again lands him in hot water with Marge and he needs to fathom a way to save his marriage, while a dose of "A Streetcar Named Desire" drives Milhouse to affect a Marlon Brando-esque bad-boy demeanor in an effort to impress Lisa.
